commentary_text: The sportsbook consensus assigns England an 81.6% win probability versus the model's 63.3%—an 18-point gap favoring the market's confidence in an England victory. This sharper conviction could reflect recent lineup news, injury updates, or current form information unavailable to the model at its last update. The market also prices Panama's upset chances at 6.1% against the model's 11.9%, suggesting bookmakers may have specific intelligence dampening Panama's prospects. Both sources agree England is the clear favorite on a neutral ground, but the gap signals the market has incorporated newer intelligence that strengthens that case beyond what the model's rating-based forecast captures.
